title: Egress firewall (iptables CLAWHQ_FWD chain)
intent: Enforce that no egress traffic leaves the agent container except to explicitly allowlisted domains. The privacy promise is architectural, not advisory.
description: PRIVACY PROMISE: The egress firewall is what makes 'zero data leaves your machine by default' true, not marketing. Without it, an agent tool or skill could call out to arbitrary domains. Per-integration domain allowlist means the user's email agent can reach email APIs and nothing else. Air-gap mode is the paranoid user's kill switch. Acceptance: docker bridge traffic blocked to non-allowlisted domains; DNS and HTTPS to allowlist work; firewall reapplied after compose down; 'clawhq doctor' verifies rules are active.
steps: Create dedicated iptables chain CLAWHQ_FWD: ESTABLISHED/RELATED -> DNS -> HTTPS allowlist -> LOG + DROP
Derive per-integration domain allowlist from config
Implement air-gap mode (block all egress)
Make apply/remove idempotent
Add verification with expected vs actual rule diff
Implement auto-reapply after docker compose down
acceptanceCriteria: Docker bridge traffic blocked to non-allowlisted domains
DNS and HTTPS to allowlisted domains work correctly
Firewall reapplied automatically after compose down
'clawhq doctor' verifies firewall rules are active
groomingNotes: SECOND GROOMING (updated) — failed once (reason: implementation failed, no further detail). BUG-009 (yaml parser fix) is ALREADY DONE — do not re-do it. IMPLEMENTATION CHECKLIST: (1) Create src/secure/firewall/firewall.ts — move applyFirewall()/removeFirewall() from src/build/launcher/firewall.ts, add verifyFirewall() that diffs expected vs actual iptables rules. (2) Add air-gap mode flag: skip allowlist, drop all outbound except ESTABLISHED/RELATED. (3) Detect Docker bridge interface dynamically via 'docker network inspect' — never hardcode. (4) Compose-down watcher for auto-reapply. (5) EXEC_DRY env var for test dry-run mode — iptables calls must be mockable. (6) Doctor integration: add verifyFirewall() check to existing src/operate/doctor/ check array. (7) sudo/privilege handling: detect non-root and surface actionable error. SUDO NOTE: iptables requires root — document requirement in error messages. TESTING: All tests must use EXEC_DRY=1 or a mock exec to avoid requiring root in CI. ACCEPTANCE CHECK: applyFirewall() idempotent (run twice = same rules); verifyFirewall() returns diff when rules are missing; air-gap mode drops all outbound; doctor check reports fail with fix message when rules absent. POST-COMPLETION BATCH: After CQ-005 sprint completes successfully, immediately mark CQ-041, BUG-020, and BUG-023 as readyForSprint — they are all blocked exclusively on this item.
